intended_use: DASI Dimensions is a standalone, non-invasive, clinical decision support software solution that is intended for use by cardiologists and radiologists in context of the aortic stenosis population. DASI
intended_use_full: DASI Dimensions is a standalone, non-invasive, clinical decision support software solution that is intended for use by cardiologists and radiologists in context of the aortic stenosis population. DASI Dimensions provides the end-user with pre-defined images and measurements (diameters, lengths, angles, areas, and perimeters) of cardiovascular structures.
target_condition: Aortic stenosis in consideration for transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR)
ground_truth: Manual measurements generated by qualified DASI Dimensions operators and clinician truthers; measurements averaged from 2 qualified truthers per case with adjudication if significant variance; current standard of care clinical measurement methods
other_metrics: AI control point detection success rate: 75.3% (training/validation/testing datasets) and 85.3% (performance validation); Primary measurements ≤15% error in ≥95% of cases; Secondary measurements ≤20% error in ≥95% of cases; Tertiary measurements (aortic valve angle) ≤25% error in ≥95% of cases; Inter-operator ICC: 0.96 for annulus area with ≤10% difference from clinician measurements in ≥95% of cases
